vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: The remediation consists of adding a certmap section to /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and restarting the sssd service. This is a straightforward configuration edit that does not modify bootloader, partitions, or critical system services, and it does not directly affect SSH or console login mechanisms, making it low risk for lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential misconfiguration could cause certificate‑based authentication failures; Incorrect syntax may prevent sssd from starting
